steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: The blank separates two independent clauses: many are still well known and Koulsy Lamko, for example, is a celebrated playwright from Chad.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: Rule: a colon after a complete independent clause introduces an example or elaboration. The first clause "many are still well known" is complete; "Koulsy Lamko, for example, is a celebrated playwright from Chad" gives a specific example. Reconstructed sentence with each choice: A) "well known," — a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses; creates a comma splice; eliminated. B) "well known:" — colon follows a complete clause and introduces an illustrative example; correct. C) "well known and" — "and" would join clauses but removes the colonic elaboration signal, making the example feel like a list item rather than an illustration; also creates an awkward run-on; eliminated. D) "well known" — no punctuation between two independent clauses creates a run-on sentence; eliminated. Verdict: B is correct.
